title: simaroubaceae
intro: The Simaroubaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the quassia or sandalwood family. This family consists of approximately 55 species of trees and shrubs, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions of the world, including Asia and the Americas. The members of this family are often recognized by their distinctive leaf shapes and aromatic wood. The Simaroubaceae family has been subject to various classifications over the years, with some sources grouping it under the Rutaceae or Burseraceae families.
